sample.title: The Gods Must Be Crazy II
sample.plot: Xixo returns. This time, his children accidentally stow away on a fast-moving poachers' truck, unable to get off, and Xixo sets out to rescue them. Along the way, he encounters a couple of soldiers trying to capture each other and a pilot and passenger of a small plane, each having their own problems.

sample.title: The Gods Must Be Crazy
sample.plot: A Sho in the Kalahari desert encounters technology for the first time--in the shape of a Coke bottle. He takes it back to his people, and they use it for many tasks. The people start to fight over it, so he decides to return it to the God--where he thinks it came from. Meanwhile, we are introduced to a clumsy biologist, a schoolteacher assigned to a small village, and a despotic revolutionary.

sample.title: Palm Trees in the Snow
sample.plot: Killian is a young man who has never left the remote mountains of Huesca (Spain) which saw him grow up. In 1953, he travels back to the exotic Equatorial African island of Fernando Poo, in Spanish Guinee, where he was born, to work in a cacao plantation alongside his father and his brother. During 20 years in this island, until the troubled days of independence, he will undertake a journey towards maturity and knowledge, but will also have to deal with pain and loss.

sample.title: Emanuelle and the White Slave Trade
sample.plot: While attempting to interview an elusive gangster, photojournalist Emanuelle notices a man pushing a girl in a wheelchair through the airport. Later, in another country she sees the same man and the girl up and walking. Intrigued, she does a bit of investigating and uncovers an organization dealing in the buying and selling of young women. She goes undercover into the organization but finds that getting out again could cost her her career...and her life.

sample.title: 7 Days in Entebbe
sample.plot: In July 1976, an Air France flight from Tel-Aviv to Paris via Athens was hijacked and forced to land in Entebbe, Uganda. The Jewish passengers were separated and held hostage in demand to release many terrorists held in Israeli prisons. After much debate, the Israeli government sent an elite commando unit to raid the airfield and release the hostages.

sample.title: Desert Flower
sample.plot: The autobiography of a Somalian nomad circumcised at 3, sold in marriage at 13, fled from Africa a while later to become finally an American supermodel and is now at the age of 38, the UN spokeswoman against female genital mutilation (FGM).

sample.title: Escape from Pretoria
sample.plot: Based on the real-life prison break of two political captives, Escape From Pretoria is a race-against-time thriller set in the tumultuous apartheid days of South Africa.

sample.title: Cry Freedom
sample.plot: Donald Woods (Kevin Kline) is chief editor of the liberal newspaper "Daily Dispatch" in South Africa. He has written several editorials critical of the views of Steve Biko (Denzel Washington). But after having met him for the first time, he changes his opinion. They meet several times, and this means that Woods and his family get attention from the Security Police. When Steve Biko dies in Police custody, he writes a book about Biko. The only way to get it published is for Woods to illegally escape the country.

sample.title: Seal Team
sample.plot: After his best friend is killed in a shark attack, Quinn, a lovable yet tenacious seal assembles a SEAL TEAM to fight back against a gang of sharks overtaking the neighborhood. But this merry band of international seals are not at all trained for such a mission. They seek the help of a much more skillful combatant, Claggart, but even his tricks and flips can't whip these guys into shape. However, with a little bit of ingenuity, intelligence and a lot of heart, our SEAL TEAM may actually be able to bring peace back to their undersea community.

sample.title: The Red Sea Diving Resort
sample.plot: Inspired by remarkable true life rescue missions, this is the incredible story of a group of Mossad agents and brave Ethiopians who in the early 80s used a deserted holiday retreat in Sudan as a front to smuggle thousands of refugees to Israel. The undercover team carrying out this mission is led by the charismatic Ari Kidron and courageous local Kabede Bimro.

sample.title: Sahara
sample.plot: Retired US admiral Sandecker's foundation finances various projects worldwide, including high tech marine salvage by brilliant Dirk Pitt's US Navy Seal veterans team including buddy Al Giordino, which dreams of finding the mysteriously missing Confederal gold aboard the ironclad battleship 'of Death'. Pitts bumps into evidence for his theory it crossed the Atlantic up the river Niger, where the admiral has an environmental project. Alas, it's a West African dictatorship where the ruthless president suppresses the desert people, in league with billionaire French energy industrialist Yves Massarde. Saving reckless WHO epidemics researcher Dr. Eva Rojas, he learns the secret abducted tribal slaves-run waste plant's toxic output threatens, through an underground fluvial system, to pollute the ocean and hence cause a global killer epidemic.

sample.title: The Last King of Scotland
sample.plot: Having graduated recently from the University of Edinburgh, the Scottish doctor, Nicholas Garrigan, flies to Uganda to work at a missionary clinic run by Dr David Merrit and his wife, Sarah. Before long, Garrigan has a chance encounter with the new President, General Idi Amin, who, right from the start, feels an immediate sympathy for him. As one thing leads to another, after a while, Idi Amin invites Nicholas to become his physician and modernise Uganda's health care system: a once-in-a-lifetime offer that the doctor cannot refuse. However, more and more, Garrigan finds himself trapped in the moral abyss of Idi Amin's murderous megalomania, putting his very soul at risk. When Nicholas finally summons up the courage to rise above the madness, he becomes embroiled in a desperate fight for survival.

sample.title: Silverton Siege
sample.plot: Following a high speed car chase with the police after a thwarted sabotage mission, the trio end up seeking refuge in a South African bank in Silverton, Pretoria. Taking the bank and its customers hostage and demanding the release of Nelson Mandela in exchange for release of the prisoners, the stakes and tensions run high.

sample.title: George of the Jungle
sample.plot: Baby George got into a plane crash in a jungle, stayed alive and was adopted by a wise ape. Ursula Stanhope, US noble woman is saved from death on safari by grown-up George, and he takes her to jungle to live with him. He slowly learns a rules of human relationships, while Ursula's lover Lyle is looking for her and the one who took her. After they are found, Ursula takes George to the USA.

categories.label: new nations under strongman rule
categories.description: Across much of the continent, recently independent states are marked by personal rule, military men in office, and governments that treat the state as the property of one circle. What you'll see: presidential motorcades, uniforms in government buildings, portraits of rulers, sudden decrees, and fear around official notice.

categories.label: apartheid ordering South African life
categories.description: In southern Africa, law and police power enforce racial separation in movement, housing, work, schooling, and political rights. What you'll see: passes and checkpoints, segregated streets and institutions, township life under surveillance, prison cells, and the blunt presence of Security Police.

categories.label: liberation struggle crossing borders
categories.description: Southern Africa is bound together by guerrilla war, exile networks, neighbouring sanctuaries, and states pulled into each other's conflicts. What you'll see: border posts, bush fighting, military patrols, safe houses, coded meetings, and roads where civilians and soldiers meet uneasily.

categories.label: airports and airfields as front lines
categories.description: Long distances and weak overland links make aircraft vital, while hijackings, rescues, and covert departures turn runways into political ground. What you'll see: old terminals, prop planes and passenger jets, control towers, military transports, runway floodlights, and tense waits on the tarmac.

categories.label: foreign powers working through local regimes
categories.description: Cold War rivalry and older imperial habits keep outside states deeply involved through advisers, arms, trade, intelligence, and favored rulers. What you'll see: expatriate compounds, foreign accents in cabinet rooms, imported weapons, embassy channels, and local crises shaped by distant capitals.

categories.label: mission clinics and thin public services
categories.description: Health care, schooling, and administration are often patchy, with missionary stations, aid projects, and a few overstretched officials filling the gaps. What you'll see: rural dispensaries, crowded wards, chalkboards in village schools, battered Land Rovers, and long journeys for basic treatment.

categories.label: cash-crop estates after colonial rule
categories.description: Plantation agriculture and export farming remain central, preserving colonial hierarchies of landholding, labour discipline, and dependence on distant markets. What you'll see: cacao fields and drying yards, overseers' houses, labour lines, port traffic, and the lingering authority of settler families.

categories.label: desert and bush beyond state reach
categories.description: Large stretches of Kalahari, savannah, and scrub lie far from firm government control, where local custom matters more than official rule. What you'll see: tracking on dry ground, isolated wells, hunting and herding, rough tracks, scattered homesteads, and sudden encounters with trucks or aircraft.

categories.label: old custom meeting imported goods
categories.description: Many communities live within older rhythms of kinship and custom while outside goods, machines, and habits arrive unevenly and alter relations. What you'll see: bottles and radios treated as novelties, barter beside cash purchase, village gatherings, handmade tools next to engines, and comic or painful misunderstanding.

categories.label: women constrained by patriarchal custom
categories.description: In many settings, marriage, sexuality, and bodily control are governed by male authority, family bargaining, and custom that leaves women with little say. What you'll see: bride arrangements, domestic labor, ritual cutting, guarded female mobility, and the risks faced by women traveling alone.

categories.label: smuggling routes for people and contraband
categories.description: Weak borders, war, and want create routes that carry refugees, poached goods, trafficked women, and clandestine passengers across seas, deserts, and frontiers. What you'll see: false papers, lorries on back roads, hidden rooms, fishing boats and cargo craft, bribed officials, and night crossings.
